_meta.purpose: Coarse EBITDA-margin table used ONLY in the n_band chain (revenue per size class -> implied EBITDA). Not used for scoring.
_meta.primary_source: Damodaran (NYU Stern), 'Margins by Sector (US)', column 'EBITDA/Sales', Last Updated January 2026. Raw copy: raw/damodaran_margin.html (see raw/sources.json for URL + retrieval date).
_meta.selection_rule: Chosen by NAICS prefix, longest match wins (selected 3-digit overrides 2-digit sector default). Values fixed from the named source before any derived output was computed (no tuning).
_meta.caveat: Damodaran margins are public-company aggregates; small private firms in the $1-10M EBITDA band typically run leaner. Every n_band is therefore quality=ESTIMATE by construction.
_meta.note_ranges: NAICS ranges 31-33, 44-45, 48-49 are expanded to their 2-digit prefixes below.

211.source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Oil/Gas (Production and Exploration) EBITDA/Sales 43.21%

31.source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Food Processing EBITDA/Sales 15.25% (dominant subsector of NAICS 31)

32.source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Paper/Forest Products EBITDA/Sales 14.65% (mid-range for wood/paper/plastics)

445.source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Retail (Grocery and Food) EBITDA/Sales 5.40%

49.source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Transportation EBITDA/Sales 9.83% (couriers/warehousing folded into transport aggregate)

51.source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Information Services EBITDA/Sales 17.28% (conservative sector default)

513.source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Software (System & Application) EBITDA/Sales 35.93% (NAICS 2022 513 = publishing incl. software)

52.source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Financial Svcs. (Non-bank & Insurance) EBITDA/Sales 21.02%. Note: Damodaran reports 0.00% for banks (EBITDA not meaningful); bank codes inherit this non-bank default and are flagged ESTIMATE like all n_band values.

523.source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Investments & Asset Management EBITDA/Sales 11.65%

53.source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Real Estate (Operations & Services) EBITDA/Sales 3.96% (agency/brokerage/property-management economics, dominant in firm counts)

532.source: ESTIMATE: rental & leasing is asset-heavy with no clean Damodaran bucket (REITs 43.79% overstates operating rental); 30% chosen as a round mid-point before deriving results

54.source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

55.source: ESTIMATE: management of companies / holding offices has no market analog; 15% placeholder chosen before deriving results

56.source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

562.source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Environmental & Waste Services EBITDA/Sales 20.99%

62.source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Hospitals/Healthcare Facilities EBITDA/Sales 15.80% (closest named bucket for provider economics; public support-services bucket at 3.87% reflects a different business model)

624.source: ESTIMATE: social assistance runs thin, largely grant/Medicaid-funded; 8% placeholder chosen before deriving results

81.source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

92.source: ESTIMATE: public administration — EBITDA is not meaningful for government units; margin 0 makes the n_band chain yield 0 by construction (recorded as a known gap)
